- No more duplicate confirmation messages

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix autocomplete: use fetch ID instead of query comparison for stale guard

The previous lastQuery check was too aggressive — every keystroke updated
lastQuery, causing debounced responses to be discarded as 'stale' even
when they were the most recent fetch. This made autocomplete appear to
wait until typing stopped, and Nominatim results (Barclays Center, etc.)
were silently dropped.

Now uses an incrementing fetchId that only changes when a new fetch
actually fires after debounce, so results are shown promptly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix/deploy staging no pr trigger (#81)

* fix: correct template expression spacing in secret availability check

Add missing space after opening braces in secrets.GH_APP_ID reference
for consistent YAML parsing across both secret checks.

* Initial plan

* fix: revert cla.yml job id from cla back to cla_assistant

Co-authored-by: jenperret <237345776+jenperret@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: retrigger checks for PR #56

* Revise governance layer description in README

Updated the description of the governance layer to emphasize the need for an agent team that aligns with team trust and compliance requirements.

* fix: Update CLA workflow to use GH_CE_* naming convention for staging

- Replace GH_APP_ID with GH_CE_APP_ID (2 occurrences)
- Replace GH_APP_PRIVATE_KEY with GH_CE_APP_PRIVATE_KEY (2 occurrences)
- Ensures naming consistency with staging branch deployment standard
- Maintains Community Edition secret naming convention across all workflows

* fix: only deploy staging on push, not on PR events

- Remove pull_request trigger to prevent environment protection errors
- PRs will build/test only; deploy happens after merge to staging branch
- Simplifies workflow by removing merged PR condition check
- Resolves 'refs/pull/*/merge not allowed to deploy' errors

Fixes #76 environment protection issue

* fix: remove branch naming requirement for PRs into staging

- Only enforce 'staging -> main' promotion rule
- Allow any branch name to create PRs into staging
- Keeps main protected (only staging can merge to main)
- Resolves PR #77 branch policy validation failures

Fixes: https://github.com/AgentCraftworks/AgentCraftworks-CE/actions/runs/22786580070

---------

Co-authored-by: AICraftWorks <jenp@outlook.com>
Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: jenperret <237345776+jenperret@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: optimize code quality, add tests, wire up YAML AI suggestions

- fix(pod_service): break on first failing container state for error_type
  detection; 'Pending' phase now only used when no container error found
- fix(diagnose_service): add db.rollback() on commit failure to prevent
  session corruption
- perf(ai-engine/main): introduce module-level AIDiagnoser singleton to
  avoid Ollama is_available() probe on every request
- feat(ai-engine): add suggest(prompt) method to AIDiagnoser for raw
  prompt invocation; expose /suggest HTTP endpoint in ai-engine/main.py
- feat(yaml_service): wire up YAML_SCAN_PROMPT_TEMPLATE to populate
  ai_suggestions in YamlScanResponse (HTTP + local-import fallback)
- chore(gemini_analyzer): migrate from deprecated google-generativeai to
  google-genai SDK; update requirements.txt; default model gemini-2.0-flash
- test: add 11 new test cases (45 total, up from 34)
  - CronJob container scanning
  - run-as-root / no-liveness-probe / no-readiness-probe rules
  - Gemini fallback routing + suggest() graceful degradation
  - diagnose history endpoints (GET /history, /history/{pod_name}, 422)
  - fix client fixture to use TestClient context manager for lifespan/init_db

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[Repo Assist] Prepare release 8.3.0 (#462)

🤖 *This PR was created by Repo Assist, an automated AI assistant.*

Prepares the RELEASE_NOTES.md for version **8.3.0** — a minor release
capturing significant improvements merged since 8.2.0 (February 24).

## Changes since 8.2.0

| Type | PR | Description |
|------|----|-------------|
| 🐛 Bug fix | #432 | Fix custom attributes on nested erased types |
| 🐛 Bug fix | #458 | Fix `GetNestedType` on
`TypeSymbol`/`ProvidedTypeSymbol` for generic provided types |
| 🐛 Bug fix | #459 | Fix mutable variable captures in
`QuotationSimplifier` — promote to ref cells |
| ⚡ Performance | #443 | Memoize `transType` in `AssemblyCompiler` to
reduce redundant type translation |
| ⚡ Performance | #457 | Cache `transTypeRef` and `transMethRef` in
assembly compiler |
| ✨ Feature | #428 | New warning when all static parameters in a type
provider are optional |
| 📚 Docs | #455 | Documentation guide overhaul |
| 🧪 Tests | #442 | Add coverage tests and Coverage build target |
| 🔧 Toolchain | #431 | Update to .NET 8 SDK and toolchain |

This is a minor version bump (8.2.0 → 8.3.0) due to the new feature
(#428) and significant improvements. If preferred, a patch release
(8.2.1) is also reasonable given the emphasis on bug fixes.

## Test Status

This PR only modifies RELEASE_NOTES.md. The build/test status for the
underlying changes is tracked in the individual PRs listed above (all
passed CI before merging).

---

*To release: merge this PR, then tag `v8.3.0` and publish the NuGet
package via the existing build pipeline.*

commit.message:
[Repo Assist] Add isStruct convenience parameter to ProvidedTypeDefinition (#464)

🤖 *This PR was created by Repo Assist, an automated AI assistant.*

Adds an optional `?isStruct: bool` parameter to both public constructors
of `ProvidedTypeDefinition`. This is a **Task 10 improvement** that
makes generating struct value types more discoverable and convenient.

## Motivation

Creating a generative struct type provider currently requires knowing to
set `baseType = Some typeof(System.ValueType)` explicitly:

```fsharp
// Before: easy to forget the right base type
let t = ProvidedTypeDefinition("Point", Some typeof(System.ValueType), isErased = false)
```

With this change, users can use a named parameter that mirrors the
existing `isInterface`, `isAbstract`, `isSealed` pattern:

````fsharp
// After: explicit and self-documenting
let t = ProvidedTypeDefinition("Point", None, isStruct = true, isErased = false)
```

The traditional approach still works unchanged (full backward
compatibility).

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/ProvidedTypes.fs` | Add `isStruct` to `defaultAttributes`; add
`?isStruct: bool` to both public constructors |
| `src/ProvidedTypes.fsi` | Update constructor signatures to expose
`?isStruct: bool` |
| `tests/GenerativeStructProvisionTests.fs` | 4 new tests:
`IsValueType`, `IsSealed`, `SequentialLayout`, traditional-approach |
| `tests/FSharp.TypeProviders.SDK.Tests.fsproj` | Include new test file
|

## Behaviour when `isStruct = true`

1. `TypeAttributes.SequentialLayout` is added to the type attributes
(matches what the F# and C# compilers emit for struct types by default)
2. The base type is set to `Some typeof(System.ValueType)` when `None`
is passed — this is what drives `IsValueType` detection, the IL type
classification, and the assembly writer's `SetParent` call

## Test Status

All 108 tests pass (104 existing + 4 new):

```
Passed!  - Failed: 0, Passed: 108, Skipped: 0, Total: 108, Duration: 5 s
````

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(terrain): 513x513 overlap tiles to eliminate chunk seam gaps

Root cause of dark lines at chunk boundaries:
- Tile A UV u=1.0 → source pixel x*512+511
- Tile B UV u=0.0 → source pixel (x+1)*512+0 = x*512+512
- 1 pixel gap → height discontinuity → visible seam

Fix: 513×513 tiles where pixel 512 = first pixel of next tile.
Now Tile A at u=1.0 and Tile B at u=0.0 sample the same source pixel.

- tiling.js: 513×513 tiles with overlap (TILE_SIZE+1 stride)
- DataTexture: 513×513 (TW = tileSize+1)
- HeightmapDecoder: count = (tileSize+1)^2
- ChunkWorkerPool: slot = (tileSize+1)^2
- WorldManager.getHeightAt: stride TW=513, range 0..T (512)
- WorldManager._buildSkirt: stride TW=513
- terrain.vert.glsl: texel 1/513

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: remove activity watchdog that kills working agents

The activity watchdog was terminating Copilot SDK sessions that appeared
idle but were actually working (waiting for tool responses, running long
builds, or processing large codebases). This caused items to fail with
'Activity timeout: no output for Xs' even when the agent was actively
working.

Changes:
- Remove session_watchdog.py (activity watchdog implementation)
- Remove watchdog setup/cancel logic from copilot_sdk.py
- Remove activity_timeout handling from sdk_helpers._check_early_exit
- Remove watchdog_abort from sdk_helpers._await_completion
- Remove ActivityWatchdogConfig from config.py
- Add idle_timeout_seconds as a top-level ProjectConfig field (migrated
  from the removed ActivityWatchdogConfig)
- Add backward-compat migration: config files with activity_watchdog key
  will have idle_timeout_seconds extracted before strict parsing
- Remove all watchdog tests from test files

The existing per-item timeout (max_timeout / 7200s default) is sufficient
protection against truly stuck sessions.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(ml): fix 5 critical disposed-tensor-return bugs in PPO and VAE

PPO ActorNetwork.forward(), CriticNetwork.forward(), and ComputeActorLoss()
used 'using var' on the final return tensor, causing it to be disposed before
the caller could access it. Same pattern in VAE VaeLoss() and ComputeKLDivergence().

Removed 'using var' from return tensors — callers own disposal via their own
'using var' at the call site. Intermediate tensors remain properly disposed.

fix: use correct commit SHA for codeql-action/upload-sarif

The previous SHA (7aefa1c) was an annotated tag object SHA, not a commit SHA.
The OpenSSF Scorecard API rejects tag SHAs as 'imposter commits'.
Updated to the actual commit SHA (c793b71) that the tag points to.

commit.message:
fix(backtest): Add spread simulation to CounterfactualBacktestService

AUDIT FIX 2026-07: CounterfactualBacktestService was the only backtesting
service (out of 5) not applying spread simulation. Since this service feeds
ML training with R-Multiple values from simulated missed opportunities,
the inflated (spread-free) R-Multiples caused the model to learn overly
optimistic profit expectations.

Changes:
- Add TradeSimulationConfig parameter to RunBacktestAsync (optional, defaults
  to BacktestHelper.CreateDefaultSimulationConfig() which enables spread)
- Apply spread adjustment to entry prices in SimulateTradeOutcome, matching
  the pattern used by BacktestingService, CausalBacktestingService, and
  AnalyticsCausalBacktestingService
- Move isLong declaration before spread calculation block

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(orchestration): deadlock prevention in PaperTradingService.TryAcquireSignalLock

Eliminated nested lock ordering violation (_signalLockGuard -> _accountLock)
by snapshotting open positions outside the signal lock guard first.
